reasoning: The primary statistical anchor from FRED for EXPINF5YR estimates a 0.44 probability of it exceeding the current value by December. Daily market indicators (T5YIE / 5-Year TIPS Breakeven Rate) experienced a short peak in late October at ~2.42% but trended downwards into early November (2.36% by Nov 7), providing downward momentum for the near-term December resolution. Last year showed significant increases in Q1 (hitting 2.49% by Feb before falling in March), likely driven by the unadjusted nature of the series capturing start-of-year CPI jumps. While this seasonal dynamic hints at a potential rise by February, overarching inflation and rate cut expectations limit the upside. As required, uncertainty increases over the horizon, pulling the February estimate closer to 0.5.
